title: Password hashes leaked in account listing response
description: The receiveAccountListUsers handler includes password hashes in the 'wired.account.password' field of the response for every user account. Any user with 'wired.account.account.list_accounts' privilege can retrieve all password hashes. This enables offline brute-force attacks against all user accounts. While the passwords are hashed (SHA-256), including them in listing responses is an unnecessary information disclosure.
recommendation: Do not include password hashes in account listing responses. If a client needs to know whether a password is set, send a boolean flag instead of the actual hash. Only return the hash (if absolutely necessary) in the read_user response, and only to users with edit_users privilege.
fix: Removed wired.account.password field from receiveAccountListUsers and accountUserMessage response builders (same fix as FINDING_A_003)
